info.title: API Evangelist Governance & Discovery API
info.description: The API Commons governance and discovery tools — lint, coverage, waivers, reporting, certification, agent export, pipeline audit, MCP/skill forging, consumer gating, reusability, and experience mapping — as one hosted REST API. Every endpoint runs the same engine the browser tools at apicommons.org/tools run, server-side. Discovery-grade linting is free; the capabilities that generate artifacts, certify, audit repos, or roll up across many APIs are Pro. Free tier is keyless (5 req/s, 1,000/day, one document per call). Send a Pro key in the x-api-key header to unlock the Pro endpoints, SARIF output, and bulk input. Get a key at https://api.apievangelist.com/v1/auth/login.

tags.name: Governance
tags.description: Lint, coverage, waivers, reporting, certification, agent export, pipeline audit, toolsmith, context gate.

tags.name: Discovery
tags.description: Reusability and experience (DX/AX) analysis across a set of APIs.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: GitHub sign-in, API key, and Stripe self-serve billing.

post.description: Lint an OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, Arazzo, or JSON Schema document against the curated 574-rule best-of-breed ruleset (Spectral). Pass your own Spectral ruleset to override the catalog.

document.description: The artifact as YAML or JSON text.

ruleset.description: An optional full Spectral ruleset definition to run instead of the catalog.

tags.description: Only run catalog rules carrying one of these tags.

example.document:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Widget API
  version: 1.0.0
paths: {}

post.description: How much of an API description the ruleset actually inspects — coverage by section, dead rules, and each rule reach.

document.description: The API description (YAML or JSON text, or an object).

rules.description: Rule definitions to measure; defaults to the curated catalog for the format.

post.description: Suppress sanctioned violations and surface expired, stale, and expiring waivers — the effective failing set your build should gate on.

waivers.description: A waivers file (YAML/JSON text) or an array of waiver objects.

post.summary: Render a governance report (FREE HTML, Pro SARIF)
post.description: Turn Spectral findings into a self-contained HTML governance report. Pass ?format=sarif for SARIF 2.1.0 output (Pro).

totals.description: Positive-rule sidecar for the compliance scoreboard.

200.description: HTML report (text/html) or SARIF log (application/json with ?format=sarif).

post.description: Re-verify a tamper-evident API governance certificate against the API it attests to. Consumer-facing trust check.

api.description: The API description object the certificate was issued over.

post.description: Inventory the PII, secret, and sensitive fields an OpenAPI exposes — across parameters and request/response schemas. Uses the same classifiers as Context Gate.

post.summary: Detect breaking changes between two versions (FREE)
post.description: Compare two OpenAPI versions and classify every change as breaking, non-breaking, or an addition — at path, operation, parameter, request-body, and response granularity.

old.description: The previous OpenAPI (YAML/JSON text or object).

new.description: The new OpenAPI (YAML/JSON text or object).

200.description: Diff result with a compatible flag, summary, and changes[].

post.description: Roll up a 0-100 maturity score with a letter grade across dimensions — design/style (lint), governance coverage, documentation completeness, and (from an apis.json) discoverability, agent-readiness, and operations.

document.description: An OpenAPI (YAML/JSON text) for the lint/coverage/documentation dimensions.

200.description: Overall score, grade, and per-dimension breakdown.

post.description: Report every deprecated operation and whether it announces a sunset (x-sunset date or a Sunset/Deprecation response header), plus deprecated schema fields — the honest migration picture.

200.description: Deprecated operations, sunset posture, and deprecated fields.

post.description: Generate an example request and success-response payload for every operation from its OpenAPI schema (author example/default/enum first, else synthesized by type/format).
Generate runnable curl / JavaScript (fetch) / Python (requests) samples for every operation, with path params filled in and a realistic request body.

baseUrl.description: Override the base URL (defaults to servers[0].url).

authHeader.description: Auth header to show, e.g. "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" or "x-api-key: $KEY".

get.description: Browse the curated best-of-breed rule catalog the validator runs — every rule with its id, format, title, severity, tags, given, and reference. Optionally filter by artifact format.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Only rules for this artifact format.

get.description: The registry of adoptable, provenanced governance rulesets — national, industry, security, and company rulesets you can extend by reference instead of running the linter defaults.

post.description: Issue a tamper-evident certificate attesting an API description passed a named ruleset at a profile threshold. A SHA-256 fingerprint lets anyone re-verify it.

results.description: The Spectral findings the certificate attests.

profile.description: One of the certification profiles (e.g. standard).

post.description: Turn a Spectral ruleset into an AGENTS.md governance block, a system-prompt instruction set, a per-rule remediation prompt pack, and a compact rule digest.

ruleset.description: A Spectral ruleset (with a rules object).

post.summary: Audit a repo Spectral CI maturity (PRO)
post.description: Score a repository Spectral governance pipeline against an 8-point maturity rubric and get a prioritized punch-list. Supply files inline, or a public repo (owner/name) to fetch.

post.summary: Forge MCP tools and Agent Skills (PRO)
post.description: From an OpenAPI, forge an MCP server design, per-operation tools, Agent Skills, an enriched OpenAPI (x-mcp / x-agent-skills), a server.json, and apis.json properties.

document.description: The OpenAPI as YAML or JSON text.

post.description: Choose which operations and fields your API exposes to agents; emit a governed Tyk OpenAPI, an MCP tool manifest, and a Spectral ruleset with PII/secret checks. Defaults to the whole surface when no selection is supplied.

200.description: Tyk OAS, MCP manifest, and ruleset.

post.description: Apply an OpenAPI Overlay (spec 1.0.0) to a document — each action resolves a target JSONPath and deep-merges update or removes the match. Returns the transformed OpenAPI plus a per-action report.

document.description: The OpenAPI to transform (YAML/JSON text or object).

overlay.description: An OpenAPI Overlay 1.0.0 document (YAML/JSON text or object).

post.description: Score how reusable a set of APIs is and detect duplication/overlap across them; optionally score apis.json composability and metadata completeness.
Validate an API Onboarding Descriptor (AID) against the api-commons AID JSON Schema, and report which optional sections it fills in (a completeness score).

descriptor.description: An API Onboarding Descriptor (object or JSON text).

post.description: Generate the agent-facing descriptors for an API: an llms.txt (well-known link summary) and an AGENTS.md consumption contract — base URL, auth, the operation table, deprecated operations to avoid, and usage rules. Reads links from an apis.json when supplied.

apisJson.description: An APIs.json whose properties supply the docs/OpenAPI/MCP/pricing links.

post.description: Map each REST operation to its MCP tool and Agent Skill (the API to MCP to Agent-Skill chain) from an APIs.json, with a free/paid coverage scorecard and an SVG flow map.

apisJson.description: An APIs.json document (with an apis array).

get.description: Start GitHub OAuth. On return you get a session cookie and a personal API key (free tier) attached to your account.
Your login, tier, API key, base URLs, and recent usage. Requires the session cookie from sign-in.

post.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for {tier: pro|business, cycle: monthly|annual}. On completion your API key is moved onto the tier usage plan.

ApiKey.name: x-api-key
ApiKey.description: A Pro or Team key from sign-in. Omit for keyless free-tier access.

Unprocessable.description: The document could not be processed.

PaymentRequired.description: This capability requires a Pro plan.
